title: oxytropis lagopus var. conjugans
intro: Oxytropis lagopus var. conjugans, also known as the white mountain locoweed, is a perennial legume plant native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Oxytropis lagopus var. conjugans include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
